executiveSummary:
# Executive Summary: Medicaid Program Performance Analysis

The national Medicaid program demonstrates signs of stabilization following pandemic-era enrollment expansions, with 74.9 million enrollees across all reporting states as of February 2026. However, significant year-over-year enrollment declines are evident across major states, ranging from North Carolina's flat performance (0.0% change) to Michigan's substantial 7.6% decrease. California experienced the largest absolute decline with a 6.8% reduction affecting over 900,000 enrollees, while Texas saw a more moderate 3.8% decrease, translating to approximately 156,000 fewer enrollees. These trends likely reflect the unwinding of pandemic-era continuous enrollment provisions and return to standard eligibility redeterminations. National spending reached $215.9 billion in fiscal year 2024, with a per-enrollee average of $2,883, though this varies dramatically by state—from Florida's efficient $2,036 per enrollee to Pennsylvania's $4,363, suggesting significant variation in benefit design, population acuity, and cost management approaches.

Quality performance remains highly stratified, with the national average score of 65.6 out of 100 masking substantial interstate variation. Ten states achieved high-performer status (75 or above), led by Massachusetts (81) and Minnesota (80), while 15 states scored below 60, with Mississippi trailing at 48. Texas ranks 30th nationally with a score of 62, placing it in the moderate tier but below the national average and significantly behind leading states. This positioning is particularly notable given Texas's high managed care penetration rate of 93.3%—well above the national average of 83.2%—suggesting that managed care adoption alone does not guarantee quality outcomes. The state's per-enrollee spending of $3,014 falls above the national average, indicating potential opportunities for both cost optimization and quality improvement. Recent federal regulatory activity, including temporary enrollment moratoria and proposed changes to state-directed payments, signals continued federal oversight and potential policy shifts that may impact state program administration and financing strategies.
texasSpotlight:
## Texas Medicaid Program Analysis

Texas operates one of the nation's largest Medicaid programs with nearly 4 million enrollees, representing the third-largest state program behind California and New York. The state's enrollment decline of 3.8% year-over-year aligns closely with the national trend as states complete post-pandemic eligibility redeterminations, performing better than peer large states like California (-6.8%) and Michigan (-7.6%). At $3,014 per enrollee, Texas maintains spending levels just above the national average of $2,883, though it spends significantly more than fellow non-expansion states like Florida ($2,036) and North Carolina ($2,623). This moderate spending level reflects Texas's strategic position of providing adequate coverage while maintaining fiscal discipline as a non-expansion state.

The program's operational strengths lie in its robust managed care infrastructure, with 93.3% penetration rates well above the national average of 83.2%, indicating strong administrative efficiency and care coordination capabilities. However, quality performance remains a key challenge, with Texas scoring 62/100 and ranking 30th nationally—placing it in the bottom half despite being the nation's second-largest state by population. This quality score significantly trails other large states and falls below the national average of 65.6, suggesting systematic issues with care delivery and health outcomes. Areas to monitor include the ongoing federal regulatory environment around state-directed payments and provider enrollment moratoria, which could impact Texas's managed care model, as well as potential opportunities to leverage the state's strong managed care penetration to drive quality improvements and close the performance gap with peer states.

alerts.title: PA per-enrollee spending 51% above national average
alerts.description: Pennsylvania's per-enrollee spending of $4,363 is significantly higher than the national average of $2,883, indicating potential cost efficiency issues.

alerts.title: Five states with critically low quality scores
alerts.description: Arkansas, South Carolina, Alabama, Oklahoma, and Mississippi all scored below 55 on quality measures, well below the national average of 65.6.

alerts.title: FL extremely low spending despite large enrollment
alerts.description: Florida's per-enrollee spending of $2,036 is 29% below national average despite being the 4th largest state by enrollment, suggesting potential underfunding.

alerts.title: MA combines highest spending with highest quality
alerts.description: Massachusetts shows $4,221 per-enrollee spending (46% above average) paired with the highest quality score of 81, demonstrating effective resource utilization.

executiveInsights.title: Texas Medicaid enrollment drops 3.8% YoY, totaling nearly 4M enrollees
executiveInsights.summary: Texas Medicaid enrollment declined to 3,997,752 enrollees in 2026-02, representing a 3.8% year-over-year decrease. This is the third-largest Medicaid program nationally but experiencing concerning enrollment losses.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Declining enrollment could signal eligibility redeterminations, economic improvements, or administrative barriers that may impact contract scope and revenue projections. HHSC needs to understand root causes to maintain program integrity.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Flag enrollment decline trends to HHSC leadership for root cause analysis

executiveInsights.title: Texas per-enrollee costs at $3,014 exceed national average by $130
executiveInsights.summary: Texas spends $3,014 per Medicaid enrollee compared to the national average of $2,883. Total Texas expenditures reached $12.05 billion, making it the 4th highest spending state.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Higher per-enrollee costs suggest either more complex case mix, higher provider rates, or potential inefficiencies that could be targets for cost management initiatives and value-based care programs.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review cost drivers analysis with HHSC finance team for optimization opportunities

executiveInsights.title: Texas quality score of 62/100 ranks #30 nationally, below high performer threshold
executiveInsights.summary: Texas scored 62 out of 100 on quality measures, ranking 30th nationally and classified as 'moderate' performance. Only 10 states achieved high performer status (≥75), while Texas falls short of this benchmark.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: Below-average quality scores could trigger CMS scrutiny, impact federal funding, and create opportunities for Accenture to propose quality improvement initiatives and performance-based interventions.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Flag quality improvement opportunities to HHSC quality team for strategic planning

executiveInsights.title: Texas managed care penetration at 93.3% significantly exceeds national average
executiveInsights.summary: Texas achieved 93.3% managed care penetration with 3.86M enrollees, well above the national average of 83.2%. This represents one of the highest managed care adoption rates nationally.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: High managed care penetration indicates strong infrastructure for value-based care initiatives and suggests Texas is well-positioned for advanced managed care reforms and innovations.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Review managed care optimization strategies with HHSC managed care division

executiveInsights.title: Multiple CMS regulatory changes active with enrollment moratoria announced
executiveInsights.summary: Recent Federal Register postings show nationwide temporary enrollment moratoria and new state directed payment regulations affecting Medicaid managed care programs. Multiple information collection activities are also proposed.
executiveInsights.whyItMatters: These regulatory changes could impact Texas operations, compliance requirements, and program design, requiring proactive assessment and potential system modifications to maintain contract compliance.
executiveInsights.actionPrompt: Flag regulatory impact assessment needs to HHSC compliance and legal teams

riskOpportunity.label: Enrollment decline stabilization
riskOpportunity.description: Texas Medicaid enrollment dropped 3.8% YoY, creating pressure for cost optimization and system efficiency improvements.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Propose enrollment analytics and retention strategies to help HHSC reverse the decline.

riskOpportunity.label: Quality performance improvement
riskOpportunity.description: Texas ranks #30 nationally with 62/100 quality score, presenting significant improvement opportunity.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Develop comprehensive quality improvement roadmap targeting top-tier performance metrics.

riskOpportunity.label: Managed care recompete
riskOpportunity.description: High 93.3% managed care penetration indicates potential major contract recompetition cycles ahead.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Monitor HHSC procurement portal actively and prepare competitive positioning for upcoming RFPs.

riskOpportunity.label: Federal regulatory changes
riskOpportunity.description: Multiple CMS rule changes on state directed payments and enrollment moratoria create compliance complexity.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Establish dedicated regulatory monitoring team to ensure HHSC compliance with evolving federal requirements.

riskOpportunity.label: OIG oversight exposure
riskOpportunity.description: Active HHS OIG Medicaid portfolio oversight creates audit risk for current operations and contracts.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Conduct proactive compliance assessment and strengthen audit readiness protocols.

riskOpportunity.label: Cost efficiency optimization
riskOpportunity.description: Texas per-enrollee cost of $3,014 vs national average $2,883 suggests efficiency improvement potential.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Propose cost reduction initiatives leveraging automation and process optimization technologies.

riskOpportunity.label: ESBD procurement pipeline
riskOpportunity.description: Texas Electronic State Business Daily shows active procurement opportunities requiring continuous monitoring.
riskOpportunity.recommendedAction: Implement automated ESBD monitoring system to identify relevant opportunities early.
